title: DirectLineConnectorOptions
description: Constructor options for DirectLineConnector — Azure Bot Framework DirectLine v3 adapter. Source: packages/connector-directline/src/DirectLineConnector.ts. Provide exactly one of: token, secret (server-side only — generates a token), or tokenGeneratorUrl.

secret.description: DirectLine channel secret. Server-side only — never expose this in browser code. The connector will exchange it for a token.

token.description: Pre-fetched DirectLine token (preferred for browser deployments).

tokenGeneratorUrl.description: URL of a backend endpoint that issues tokens. The connector will POST to this URL and expect { token, conversationId?, userId? } in the response.

userId.description: Override the user id. When omitted, a random per-instance id is generated (or persisted, if resumeConversation is true).

userName.description: Display name attached to outgoing activities. Defaults to the resolved userId.

locale.description: BCP-47 locale (e.g. tr-TR) sent in the webchat/join event and outgoing activities.

joinParameters.description: Extra key/value pairs merged into the webchat/join event's value payload.

eventHandlers.description: Map of bot-initiated event names to handler functions. Cannot be serialised — set programmatically.

resumeConversation.description: Persist conversationId, token, watermark, and the auto-generated userId to localStorage so reload resumes the same conversation.

typingTimeoutMs.description: How long the typing indicator stays visible after a bot typing signal before auto-clearing. Each new signal resets the timer.

typingUntilMessage.description: When true, the typing indicator stays on until the next bot message arrives (no auto-clear).
